Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.

original_text:
### Typography Hierarchy (fonts, weights, cases)
| Level | Print | Digital / Email | Word / PPT / Excel fallback |
|---|---|---|---|
| Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos** |
| Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos** |
| Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos |
 
- Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
- Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
- Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.
- **[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`. Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.
- **[GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height):**
  - H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
  - H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
  - H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
  - Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
  - Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
  - Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
  - Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)
- **Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).
- Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

relations.note: Type scale floor is reinforced by the never-below-12px running text rule.
Type scale line-heights instantiate the 150% minimum line-height rule.
Family selection constrains which weight set applies.

**Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case
*Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
| Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos** |
| Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos** |

rules.intent: Preserve approved brand type families per production medium.
rules.rule_text: Typography families are medium-dependent: Print headlines/headers use Agenda (headline.font = Agenda) with body copy in Nunito Sans; Digital/Email uses Nunito Sans (body.font = Nunito Sans) for headlines (bolder weights, Title Case) and body; Word/PPT/Excel fallback uses Aptos. Agenda may be used as live text in digital ONLY for the campaign headline (campaign_headline.font = Agenda). Footers and fine print use Nunito Sans (Aptos in Office fallback).

quotes: Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.

rules.intent: Constrain typography to the licensed/approved weight set.
rules.rule_text: Only the published weights may be used per family: Agenda in Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold; Nunito Sans in Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold; Aptos in Regular, Bold.

quotes: `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`

rules.intent: Ensure consistent brand font rendering with graceful cross-client fallback.

Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

rules.intent: Lock the email typographic scale and its sizing/line-height obligations.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height): H1 section hero headline 28px/36px (≈130%) Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold; H2 section heading 22px/30px (≈136%) Nunito Sans Bold; H3 subhead 18px/26px (≈144%) Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold; Body 16px/24px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular — 16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better"); Secondary/supporting body 14px/21px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular, used sparingly and never for primary content; Footnote/reference text within a section 12px/18px (150%), must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it.

quotes: Never set running text below 12px.
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Enforce a legibility floor for running text.
rules.rule_text: Never set running text below 12px (size.min.running_text = 12px). ISI sizing is owned by the locked component and out of scope for this rule.

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Maintain legible line spacing, with extra leading for light-on-dark contexts.
rules.rule_text: Line height must be a minimum of 150% for all body copy (body.line_height = 150%; brand accessibility rule). For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further — [GENERAL] use 160% (line_height.light_on_dark = 160%, e.g. 16px/26px).

quotes: Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Guarantee readable information hierarchy through distinct type treatment.
rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, and last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
